description_long: This research institute building features a modern, geometric design with a prominent red sloped roof covering the main section and a series of rectangular, white upper structures that likely house laboratories or offices. The facade includes large, continuous glass windows on the ground floor, providing ample natural light and a sense of transparency. The upper levels are characterized by horizontal strip windows and a series of open, recessed sections that may serve as terraces or ventilation shafts. The building's exterior is primarily finished in white and red, with black accents at the base, giving it a clean and contemporary appearance. The structure appears to be constructed from concrete, glass, and metal, typical of institutional architecture. There are no visible decorative elements, signage, or logos, emphasizing functionality and simplicity.
description: A modern research institute with a red sloped roof, white upper structures, and large glass windows on the ground floor. The design is clean, geometric, and functional.
description_view_0: This view highlights the red sloped roof and the white upper structures with open sections, as well as the glass facade at the base.
description_view_1: The long side of the building is visible, showcasing the extensive glass windows and the full length of the red roof, along with the recessed upper sections.
description_view_2: This angle shows the side elevation with a single strip window and the transition between the red roof and the white upper block.
description_view_3: The rear of the building is visible, featuring more strip windows and the flat roof sections, with minimal detailing.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is located along the glass facade, featuring large transparent glass doors integrated into the window wall. The entrance is wide and accessible, flush with the ground for easy wheelchair access, and lacks steps or decorative elements.
